sections.paragraph:
προτίθημι 1 aor. προέθηκα LXX; 2 aor. subj. προθῶ; pf. ptc. προτεθεικώς (Just., D. 65, 3). Mid. 2 aor. προεθέμην. Pass.: 1 aor. ptc. gen. pl. προτεθέντων (Ath., R. 15 p. 65, 20); pf. ptc. προτεθειμένος LXX (Hom.+; ins, pap, LXX; TestReub 1:6 v.l.; Philo, Joseph.; Ar. 13, 5; Just.; Ath.).

① to set someth. before someone as someth. to be done, set before someone as a task/duty, act. w. dat. τινί (Soph., Ant. 216; Hdt. 3, 38; 9, 27) ἐὰν σὺ σεαυτῷ προθῇς ὅτι Hm 12, 3, 5.

② to set forth publicly, display publicly, make available publicly, mid. (Appian, Bell. Civ. 3, 26 §101; Just., D. 65, 3 τὸ πρόβλημα) of Christ ὸ̔ν προέθετο ὁ θεὸς ἱλαστήριον Ro 3:25 (s. ἱλαστήριον). But the act., at least, seems to have had the mng. offer as well (s. SIG 708, 15 w. the editor’s note 5; 714, 16–18, and M-M.; also ZPE 3, ’68, 166 n. 9).

③ to have someth. in mind beforehand, plan, propose, intend, τὶ someth., mid. (Pla., Phdr. 259d; Polyb. 6, 12, 8; Jos., Vi. 290) Eph 1:9. W. inf. foll. (Pla., Rep. 1, 352d, Leg. 1, 638c; Polyb. 8, 13, 3; 11, 7, 3; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 287, Ant. 18, 286; 19, 37) Ro 1:13 (B-D-F §392, 1a). ὁ καιρὸς ὸ̔ν θεὸς προέθετο φανερῶσαι … the time that God had appointed to reveal (as part of a comprehensive plan and design) Dg 9:2.—M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

  1. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. pl. = plural

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

TestReub TestReub = Testament of Reuben, s. Test12Patr—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

act. act. = active

dat. dat. = dative

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Hm Hm = Mandates

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

ZPE ZPE = Zeitschrift für Papyrologie und Epigraphik—Lists 3, 4, 6

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 889
